help_articles.title: Vehicle storage access hours and gate times
help_articles.summary: Learn when you can access your stored vehicle, how gate hours work, and where 24-hour access is available.
help_articles.content:
You can access vehicle storage spaces during posted gate access hours at each facility. Gate access hours may differ from office hours.

Most locations offer gate access between 6:00 AM and 10:00 PM, 7 days a week. Some facilities provide extended or 24-hour access upon request and approval.

24-HOUR ACCESS
- 24-hour access is not available at all facilities.
- Where available, it may be limited to vehicle storage customers or certain unit types.
- Additional verification and an access agreement may be required.

HOW TO CHECK ACCESS HOURS FOR A SPECIFIC LOCATION
1. Visit the location details page on our website.
2. Look for the "Access Hours" section near the top of the page.
3. If 24-hour access is available, it will be clearly labeled.
4. If it is not listed, contact the facility directly using the phone number or contact form on the location page.

VEHICLE STORAGE-SPECIFIC RULES
- Vehicles must be operable, insured, and registered in the renter’s name (or with written authorization).
- Overnight occupancy or living in a vehicle on-site is strictly prohibited.
- After-hours access, where offered, is for quick visits to drop off or pick up your vehicle only.

If you have questions about 24-hour access or after-hours vehicle access fees at a specific facility, please contact that location directly or use the contact form in our Help Center.

help_articles.title: Vehicle storage options: cars, RVs, boats, and trailers
help_articles.summary: Overview of available vehicle storage types, size suggestions, and basic requirements.
help_articles.content:
We offer several types of vehicle storage to fit different needs:

TYPES OF VEHICLE STORAGE
1. Outdoor uncovered parking
   - Most common and budget-friendly option.
   - Available in sizes such as 10x20, 10x25, and 12x40.
2. Outdoor covered parking
   - Offers shelter from sun and precipitation.
   - Ideal for RVs, boats, and trailers.
3. Enclosed drive-up units
   - Fully enclosed storage similar to a garage.
   - Available for smaller vehicles, motorcycles, and compact cars.

TYPICAL SIZE GUIDELINES
- Compact car: 10x15 or 10x20
- Sedan or small SUV: 10x20
- Large truck/SUV: 10x25
- Class C RV or boat with trailer: 12x30
- Large RV or bus: 12x35 or larger

BASIC REQUIREMENTS
- Current registration and proof of ownership.
- Valid government-issued ID.
- Proof of insurance where required by local regulations.
- Vehicle must be in good working condition (no major leaks).

Before reserving, review the specific facility details to confirm that vehicle storage is available and that your vehicle will fit the space.

help_articles.title: Access hours vs. office hours
help_articles.summary: Understand the difference between gate access hours and on-site office hours.
help_articles.content:
Each facility lists both gate access hours and office hours.

ACCESS HOURS (GATE HOURS)
- Times when renters can enter the property using a gate code or access card.
- Often 6:00 AM–10:00 PM, 7 days a week, but this varies by location.
- Some facilities offer 24-hour gate access for approved customers.

OFFICE HOURS
- Times when staff are physically on-site and available to assist.
- Usually within standard business hours (for example, 9:30 AM–6:00 PM on weekdays).
- Office hours may be shorter on weekends or holidays.

HOW TO CHECK HOURS
- On the location details page, access hours and office hours appear near the top.
- Holiday hours and special closures may be listed in the notes section.

If you need after-hours access or have questions about 24/7 availability, contact your facility directly or submit a request through our Help Center.

help_articles.title: How to reserve a storage unit online
help_articles.summary: Step-by-step guide for reserving a unit through our website with no obligation.
help_articles.content:
You can reserve a storage unit online in a few simple steps:

1. Search by city or ZIP code using the "Find Storage" search bar.
2. Filter results by unit size, features (such as drive-up or climate controlled), and promotions.
3. Click "Reserve" or "Select" next to the unit you want.
4. Choose your move-in date from the calendar.
5. Enter your contact information (name, phone, and email).
6. Select your payment preference (such as "Pay at move-in" if available).
7. Review your reservation details and click "Complete Reservation".

You will receive a confirmation email with your reservation number and instructions for move-in. Many locations allow you to change your move-in date later by contacting the facility.

help_articles.title: Can I move in on the same day I reserve?
help_articles.summary: Information about same-day reservations and move-ins.
help_articles.content:
In many cases, you can reserve and move in on the same day, subject to availability and office hours.

SAME-DAY RESERVATIONS
- Most locations allow same-day reservations online until office closing time.
- If you plan to arrive close to closing, call ahead to confirm staff availability.

ONLINE CHECK-IN
- Some facilities support online check-in and document signing.
- If available, you may be able to move in even if the office is closed, as long as gate access is active.

REQUIRED ITEMS FOR MOVE-IN
- Valid government-issued photo ID.
- Payment method for the first month’s rent and fees, if applicable.
- Proof of insurance or selection of a protection plan, where required.

Check your confirmation email for specific instructions and any facility-specific requirements.

help_articles.title: Billing and payment options
help_articles.summary: Learn about monthly billing, auto-pay, and accepted payment methods.
help_articles.content:
Our storage rentals are billed month-to-month with no long-term commitment.

BILLING CYCLE
- Rent is charged monthly on your billing date.
- You will see a prorated amount on your first bill if you move in mid-month.

PAYMENT METHODS
- Credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover)
- ACH bank payments (where available)
- Cash, check, or money order at participating locations

AUTO-PAY
- You can enroll in auto-pay during or after your reservation.
- Auto-pay helps you avoid late fees by automatically charging your card each month.

LATE FEES
- If payment is not received by the due date and grace period, late fees may apply.
- Fees and timelines vary by state; see your rental agreement for details.

help_articles.title: Security features at our storage facilities
help_articles.summary: Overview of typical security measures including cameras, gated access, and locks.
help_articles.content:
Security is a shared responsibility between our facilities and our customers.

COMMON SECURITY FEATURES
- Electronic gated access with unique access codes
- Video surveillance cameras in key areas
- Well-lit driveways and corridors
- Perimeter fencing where permitted
- Individual unit locks (customer-provided or purchased on-site)

LOCKS
- We strongly recommend high-security disk or cylinder locks.
- Some locations require a specific lock type for certain doors.

BEST PRACTICES FOR CUSTOMERS
- Never share your gate code or key with unauthorized individuals.
- Ensure your lock is fully latched and closed each time you visit.
- Report suspicious activity to the facility manager.

While we take many steps to help protect your belongings, no facility can guarantee security. This is why insurance or a protection plan for stored items is highly recommended.

help_articles.title: Do I need insurance for my stored items?
help_articles.summary: Why coverage is recommended and how our insurance and protection plans work.
help_articles.content:
We strongly recommend that all customers maintain coverage for stored items, either through an existing insurance policy or a storage-specific plan.

EXISTING COVERAGE
- Some homeowners or renters policies extend limited coverage to stored property.
- Contact your insurance provider to confirm what is covered, any exclusions, and deductibles.

FACILITY-OFFERED COVERAGE
- Many of our locations offer third-party insurance or facility-backed protection plans.
- Coverage options typically range from $1,000 to $10,000.
- Monthly premiums vary based on coverage amount.

CHOOSING A COVERAGE LEVEL
- Estimate the total replacement cost of the items you plan to store.
- Choose the next higher available coverage tier (for example, select $3,000 coverage if your estimate is around $2,500).

You can add or update coverage during the online reservation checkout flow or by contacting the facility office.

help_articles.title: How to use the storage unit size guide
help_articles.summary: Tips for choosing the right size using our size guide and calculator.
help_articles.content:
Our size guide and calculator help you choose the best unit size based on your household type and items.

USING THE SIZE CALCULATOR
1. Select your home type or number of bedrooms (for example, 1-bedroom apartment).
2. Check off items you plan to store (such as mattresses, sofas, boxes, and appliances).
3. Click "Calculate" or "Get recommendation" to see a suggested size.

COMPARING SIZES
- The guide lists standard sizes like 5x5, 5x10, 10x10, 10x15, and 10x20.
- Each card includes typical contents and example layouts.
- You can choose the recommended size or the next larger size if you prefer extra space.

If you are unsure between two sizes, we usually suggest choosing the slightly larger option for easier access and future items.

help_articles.title: How to contact customer support
help_articles.summary: Ways to reach us, including the Help Center contact form for specific questions.
help_articles.content:
If you need help with a reservation, billing question, or facility details, you can reach us in several ways:

1. HELP CENTER CONTACT FORM
- Visit the Help & FAQ page from the footer or main navigation.
- Search for an article related to your question.
- At the bottom of most articles, click "Contact us" or "Still need help?".
- Fill out the form with your name, email, topic, and message, then click "Submit".

2. CALL THE FACILITY
- On each location details page, you’ll find the local phone number.
- Call during office hours for fastest assistance with location-specific questions.

3. EMAIL
- You can reply directly to most reservation confirmation emails with additional questions.

Providing your location name, city, and unit or reservation number (if available) helps us respond more quickly.

unit_sizes.label: 5x5
unit_sizes.description: Small closet-sized space ideal for boxes, seasonal items, or small furniture.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Several medium boxes, small bookshelf, seasonal décor, or sports gear.

unit_sizes.label: 5x10
unit_sizes.description: Walk-in closet or small walk-in storage, great for a studio or 1-bedroom worth of items.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Mattress set, small sofa, dresser, TV stand, and several boxes.

unit_sizes.label: 5x15
unit_sizes.description: Narrow but deeper space, ideal for long furniture pieces or narrow household loads.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Couch, dining set, bookcases, and 10–15 medium boxes.

unit_sizes.label: 10x10
unit_sizes.description: Half of a standard one-car garage; fits contents of a typical 1–2 bedroom apartment.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Bedroom sets, sofa, appliances, dining table, and 20+ boxes.

unit_sizes.label: 10x15
unit_sizes.description: Spacious unit for larger apartments or small homes with multiple rooms.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Multiple bedroom sets, large sectional sofa, appliances, and many boxes.

unit_sizes.label: 10x20
unit_sizes.description: Standard one-car garage size, suitable for a full 2–3 bedroom home or a small vehicle.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Entire household of furniture, large appliances, or a compact car plus boxes.

unit_sizes.label: 10x25
unit_sizes.description: Deep one-car garage size for a larger home or vehicle plus storage.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: 3–4 bedroom home contents, large furniture, or vehicle with extra storage.

unit_sizes.label: 10x30
unit_sizes.description: Extra-large unit for full home moves or business inventory and equipment.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: 4–5 bedroom home, multiple sofas, appliances, or business stock and shelving.

unit_sizes.label: 12x30
unit_sizes.description: Extra-wide and deep space ideal for large households or combined home and business storage.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Large home furnishings, equipment, or multiple room sets and palletized goods.

unit_sizes.label: Outdoor Parking 10x20
unit_sizes.description: Uncovered outdoor parking space for standard cars, small trucks, or boats on trailers.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Sedan, small SUV, compact pickup, or small boat and trailer.

unit_sizes.label: Covered Parking 12x30
unit_sizes.description: Covered parking suitable for RVs, larger boats, and trailers needing overhead protection.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Class C RV, large boat on trailer, or oversized truck with trailer.

unit_sizes.label: RV Parking 12x40
unit_sizes.description: Extra-long outdoor space designed for large RVs, buses, or extended trailers.
unit_sizes.typical_contents: Class A RV, motorhome, or long boat and trailer combination.

size_guide_rules.notes: Typical studio with basic furniture and about 10 medium boxes. A 5x10 is usually sufficient if furniture can be stacked vertically.
Standard 1-bedroom with a queen bed, small sofa, dining table, TV stand, and a few boxes. Consider the next larger size if you have bulky furniture or want extra aisle space.
Fully furnished 1-bedroom with dressers and 10–15 medium boxes. A 10x10 allows easier access and space for additional items.
Typical 2-bedroom apartment including living room set, dining set, two bedroom sets, and about 20 boxes.
3+ bedroom home with large furniture, major appliances, and around 30 boxes. Consider 10x25 or larger if storing outdoor items or garage contents as well.
Small office with several desks, file cabinets, and archive boxes. Allows for narrow aisles between stacked boxes.
